name: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B
archDisplay: Hybrid-attention MoE (Qwen3-Next lineage): 48 layers, 12x(3x(Gated DeltaNet -> MoE) -> 1x(Gated Attention -> MoE)); 256 experts, 8 routed + 1 shared activated per token; hidden dim 3072; natively multimodal (Causal LM with built-in vision encoder)
note: Mid-size open-weight MoE in the Qwen3.5 family, released ~1 week after the 397B-A17B flagship alongside Qwen3.5-27B (dense) and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (MoE). Matches or beats GPT-OSS-120B and Qwen3-235B-A22B on several benchmarks at a fraction of the total/active parameter count.

specSources.releaseDate (official changelog): https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6 (README: '2026-02-24: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B, Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, and Qwen3.5-27B are released')

results.notes: beats GPT-5-mini (83.7), GPT-OSS-120B (80.8), and Qwen3-235B-A22B (84.4) baselines in the same table

Reported as 'HLE w/ CoT' (STEM & Reasoning category). A second, higher 'HLE w/ tool' score of 47.5 is also reported (Search Agent category) but there is no separate slug for the tool-augmented variant, so only the CoT number is used here to avoid ambiguity.

Elo rating, not a 0-100 score. Footnote in the README states this was evaluated on Qwen's own query set (not necessarily the standard public CodeForces problem set), so treat as vendor-reported and not perfectly apples-to-apples with third-party CodeForces evals.
Search Agent category, English Browsecomp. A separate Browsecomp-zh score of 69.9 is also reported but has no distinct slug in the catalog, so it is omitted.
Vision-Language table, STEM and Puzzle category.
Vision-Language table; README footnote notes a fixed \boxed{} prompt was used for this model's score.
Reported as 'Mathvista(mini)', the standard mini-test-set variant commonly reported as MathVista.
Visual Agent category, computer-use agent benchmark.

LMArena (Chatbot Arena) Elo, Text overall. Independently corroborated by a second aggregator citation (metatext.io, same 1417 figure) and by Arena.ai's own official X/Twitter post (x.com/arena/status/2029360146863014145) which places Qwen3.5-122b-a10b's Text Arena score at ~1420 and Code Arena score at 1384 -- close but not pixel-identical, consistent with this being a live, continuously-updating leaderboard rather than a fixed benchmark. A separate direct fetch of arena.ai/leaderboard returned a suspicious identical 1485 value for two different models (likely a scraping/rendering artifact from the JS-heavy page) and was disregarded in favor of the two independently-converging aggregator citations.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via direct fetch of its own HF model card (full architecture spec + multi-baseline benchmark table) and the official QwenLM/Qwen3.6 GitHub README changelog, which independently states the exact same release date (2026-02-24) alongside its Qwen3.5-27B and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B siblings. This was the specific model named in the original lead's github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.5 citation (that URL redirects to github.com/QwenLM/Qwen3.6, whose README covers the full 3.5+3.6 changelog including this model). No independent (non-Alibaba) benchmark corroboration was found in this pass -- all results are self-reported from the primary HF card; flagged accordingly. Not to be confused with Qwen3.5-35B-A3B or Qwen3.5-27B, its same-day-released siblings, which are real but were not part of the original flagged leads and are not added as separate candidates here (see excluded).

SUPPLEMENTARY COVERAGE PASS: Primary source: fetched https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-122B-A10B/raw/main/README.md directly via curl and verified line-by-line against the raw HTML tables (not just a WebFetch summary) — confirmed genuine, not hallucinated. The README has two large benchmark tables (Language, and Vision-Language) with ~70 total benchmark rows for Qwen3.5-122B-A10B, compared against GPT-5-mini, GPT-OSS-120B, Qwen3-235B-A22B / Claude-Sonnet-4.5, Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B, plus Qwen3.5-27B and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B siblings.

The Qwen blog (https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.5) could NOT be read — it is a client-side-rendered single-page app; both WebFetch and a direct curl with a browser user-agent returned only an empty page shell plus a huge SEO meta-keywords blob, no article/table content. No data from the blog was used; everything above comes from the HF model card, which is more granular than a typical blog post anyway (it lists ~70 distinct evals vs. the handful a blog post usually highlights).

19 additional clean-mapping results were extracted beyond the 4 already known (MMLU-Pro, MMLU-Redux, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench v6), covering knowledge, instruction-following, hard-exam (HLE), agentic coding (SWE-bench Verified, Terminal Bench 2), competitive coding (CodeForces), agentic search (Browsecomp), multilingual knowledge (MMMLU), and multimodal/vision benchmarks (MMMU, MMMU-Pro, MathVision, MathVista, MMBench-EN, CharXiv, AI2D, OCRBench, OSWorld-Verified).

Deliberately excluded as NOT clean matches (to avoid misrepresenting scores under the wrong slug):
- BFCL-V4 (72.2): catalog only has 'bfcl' and 'bfcl-v3' slugs; v4 has a different task/scoring protocol than v3, so mapping it to 'bfcl-v3' would misattribute a different benchmark version.
- TAU2-Bench (79.5): catalog only has 'tau2-bench-telecom'; the README footnote indicates this score spans multiple domains (including an 'airline' domain fix), i.e. it looks like a cross-domain aggregate, not a telecom-only score — mapping it to the telecom-specific slug would misrepresent it.
- RefCOCO(avg) (91.3): likely an average across RefCOCO/RefCOCO+/RefCOCOg variants; ambiguous which catalog slug (refcoco vs refcoco-plus) it corresponds to.
- VideoMME: reported as two separate official numbers, 'w subtitles' (87.3) and 'w/o subtitles' (83.9), with no single canonical value — picking one arbitrarily for the 'videomme' slug would be a judgment call presented as fact, so it was omitted.
- Global PIQA (88.4): this is an explicitly multilingual/adapted variant, not the standard PIQA benchmark the 'piqa' slug refers to.
- SimpleVQA (61.7): a different benchmark from 'simpleqa' in the catalog (visual-grounded QA vs. text factual QA) — not conflated despite the similar name.
- HLE w/ tool (47.5): a second, tool-augmented HLE variant; no separate slug exists for it distinct from the CoT-only 'hle' score already used, so it was dropped rather than double- or wrongly-mapped.

Many other reported benchmarks (HMMT Feb25/Nov25, AA-LCR, LongBench v2, OJBench, FullStackBench en/zh, VITA-Bench, DeepPlanning, WideSearch, Seal-0, MMLU-ProX, NOVA-63, INCLUDE, PolyMATH, WMT24++, MAXIFE, DynaMath, ZEROBench(_sub), VlmsAreBlind, BabyVision, RealWorldQA, MMStar, HallusionBench, OmniDocBench1.5, MMLongBench-Doc, CC-OCR, ERQA, CountBench, ODInW13, EmbSpatialBench, RefSpatialBench, LingoQA, Hypersim, SUNRGBD, Nuscene, VideoMMU, MLVU, MVBench, LVBench, MMVU, ScreenSpot Pro, AndroidWorld, TIR-Bench, V*, SLAKE, PMC-VQA, MedXpertQA-MM) genuinely have no corresponding slug in the given catalog at all — this is not a gap in extraction effort, the catalog simply doesn't cover these evals. Notably absent from the actual README: AIME, GSM8K, MATH, Arena-Hard, FrontierMath, Putnam, MiniF2F, USAMO, GAIA, DocVQA, ChartQA, GQA, VQAv2, COCO-Caption, Flickr30k, GDPval — none of these were evaluated/reported for this model per the vendor's own card (grep-verified absent from the full README).

One caution: a WebSearch aggregator hit (llm-stats.com / benchlm.ai) claimed an 'AIME 2026: 91.3' score for this model, but that could not be corroborated in the vendor's own README (which instead reports HMMT Feb25=91.4 / HMMT Nov25=90.3, a different math-olympiad benchmark) and referenced unfamiliar competitor model names ('GPT-5.2', 'Claude Opus 4.6'). Treated as unverifiable third-party/aggregator noise and deliberately excluded per the 'never invent a score' instruction.
